uif.source: Unemployment Insurance Contributions Act, 2002 (Act No. 4 of 2002)
uif.applies_to: All employees except: those working less than 24 hours per month, learners under Learnership Agreement contemplated in Skills Development Act, public servants under Public Service Act 1994, foreigners on contract working under permit (in certain categories)

notes.2026_changes: From January 2026, UIF system modernization announced but contribution rates and ceiling remain at R17,712/month as of effective date. Monitor Government Gazette for any mid-year amendments.
notes.calculation: UIF = min(remuneration, monthly_ceiling) * rate. Both employee and employer contribute.
notes.weekly_payroll: For weekly payroll: weekly ceiling = R17,712 / 4.333 = R4,087.74 (approximate). Use monthly ceiling pro-rated.

sdl.source: Skills Development Levies Act, 1999 (Act No. 9 of 1999)
sdl.applies_to: Employers with annual payroll exceeding R500,000. Employer-only contribution; cannot be deducted from employee salary.
exempt_employers: Employers with total annual leviable remuneration not exceeding R500,000
National or provincial public entities (certain categories)

notes.calculation: SDL = total_leviable_remuneration * 0.01. Employer bears full cost.
notes.leviable_remuneration: Includes salaries, wages, overtime, leave pay, bonuses, commissions, and allowances. Excludes reimbursive travel allowances.
